response:
Section 1  
The task requires writing a Python function to count the number of occurrences of a specific word in a given text, ignoring case sensitivity. The function must accept two parameters: `text` and `word`, and return an integer representing the count of the word in the text.

Section 2  
The function should process the text to count occurrences of the specified word, ignoring case. This involves converting both the text and the word to lowercase (or uppercase) to ensure case insensitivity. The function should then split the text into words and count how many times the specified word appears.

Section 3  
The function must be case-insensitive, meaning that it should treat "Freedom" and "freedom" as the same word. This is achieved by converting both the text and the word to lowercase (or uppercase) before comparison. The function should also handle punctuation and other non-word characters that may appear in the text.

Section 4  
The function should return the count of the specified word in the text. It should handle edge cases, such as the word not being present at all, or being present multiple times. The function should also ensure that the output is an integer and that the count is accurate even if the text contains punctuation or other non-word characters.

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections. No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet.
